Dietitian
Licensed Dietitian (LD) Guide

Issued by the Kentucky Board of Licensure and Certification for Dietitians and Nutritionists under the Department of Professional Licensing (DPL). Data verified for 2026.

Visit Official Board Portal
Fees Board Contact License Types How to Apply Documents Reciprocity FAQ
Total Initial Fee
$50
$50 initial application fee
Renewal
$50
Annual (by Oct 31st) + 15 CE hours
Processing Time
2 to 4 Weeks
Subject to board volume
License Duration
1 Year
From date of issuance
Board Contact

Kentucky Board of Licensure and Certification for Dietitians and Nutritionists

Frankfort, KY 40602

Phone & Fax

Fax: (502) 564-4818

Email

diet@ky.gov

Credential Categories

Kentucky Dietitian License Types

Licensed Dietitian (LD)

The credential to practice dietetics in Kentucky. Dual licensure as a Dietitian and Nutritionist is also an option for the same $50 application fee.

Application Process

How to Apply for a Kentucky Licensed Dietitian (LD)

Complete Education & Supervised Practice

Earn a master's degree or higher from an ACEND-accredited program. Complete a minimum of 1,000 hours of supervised practice.

Pass the CDR Examination

Successfully pass the Commission on Dietetic Registration (CDR) Registration Examination for Dietitians.

Submit the Application via eServices

Create an account on the Kentucky DPL eServices portal, submit the online application, and pay the $50 application fee.

Provide Documentation

Upload or mail a copy of your current active CDR registration card, official transcripts, and proof of supervised practice.

Required Documents

Application Document Checklist

  • CDR Registration CardProof of active registration with the Commission on Dietetic Registration.
  • Official College TranscriptsMust show your conferred master's degree. Can be sent securely via Clearinghouse to the board.
  • Supervised Practice VerificationDocumentation showing completion of at least 1,000 hours.
Practice Laws

Provisional Practice & Reciprocity

Provisional Rules

Kentucky does not offer a temporary or provisional license. Applicants must have passed the CDR examination prior to applying for licensure.

Reciprocity & Endorsement

Kentucky allows licensure by endorsement. Applicants actively licensed in another state must provide verification of licensure and proof that they meet the same educational, supervised practice, and examination requirements (active CDR registration typically satisfies this).

Frequently Asked Questions

Kentucky Dietitian FAQ

How much does a Kentucky dietitian license cost?

The application fee for initial licensure as a dietitian (or dual licensure as a dietitian/nutritionist) is $50.

When does the Kentucky license need to be renewed?

Kentucky dietitian licenses expire annually on October 31st. The renewal fee is $50. There is a 60-day grace period (until Dec 31st) with late fees applied.

What are the continuing education requirements?

Licensees must complete at least 15 hours of approved continuing education every year prior to the October 31st renewal deadline.
